perturbation.original_text: 7. Disputes and Gov erning Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. 7.2 This Agreement shall be governed by the substantive laws of Sweden.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Disputes and Gov erning Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. 7.2 This Agreement shall be governed by the substantive laws of Sweden.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce' and 'The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden' from Section 7.1, the specific procedures for arbitration are omitted. However, the clause still mandates 'arbitration,' creating a contradiction because the details of the arbitration process are now undefined. This also contradicts the governing law clause since the details are omited the governing law have no effect on these
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Disputes and Gov erning Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. 7.2 This Agreement shall be governed by the substantive laws of Sweden.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified arbitration according to the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of the Stockholm Chamber of Commerce, and outlined the composition of the arbitral tribunal and the place of arbitration. The changed text removes these specific details, only mandating 'arbitration' without defining the process. This creates a contradiction because the governing law (laws of Sweden) becomes difficult to apply when the specific procedures are undefined. The changed text contradicts the intention of a clearly defined arbitration process which is necessary for enforecement.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will survive the termination of this Agreeme nt. 8.2 The obligations under this Agreement shall apply retroactively to any information exchanged prior to the effectiveness of this Agreement. 8.3 Should the Parties enter into an agreement of the type referred in Section 1 the confidentiality provisions in s uch agreement will prevail over this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. 8.2 The obligations under this Agreement shall apply retroactively to any information exchanged prior to the effectiveness of this Agreement. 8.3 Should the Parties enter into an agreement of the type referred in Section 1 the confidentiality provisions in s uch agreement will prevail over this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will survive the termination of this Agreement', the agreement's stance on post-termination confidentiality becomes ambiguous. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 3 establishes confidentiality obligations, but now Section 8 no longer explicitly states they survive termination. This directly impacts how Section 3 will be interpreted and enforced after the initial term concludes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as confidential; ii) to take steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 8.1 explicitly stated that confidentiality obligations survive termination. The changed text omits this statement, creating a contradiction with Section 3, which outlines confidentiality obligations. Without the survival clause, it's unclear whether Section 3's confidentiality obligations continue after the agreement terminates, directly conflicting with the implied continuation in Section 3. This omission makes the enforceability of Section 3 after termination ambiguous, thus indicating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 Haldex Non -disclosure Agreement May 2015. REG NO: HDX4- 51-130 This non-disclosure agreement (the “Agreement”) is entered into by and between ______________ _____ ____, with company registration number _______ ____________, address at ___________________________ _______ and fax number _____________ ___ hereinafter referred to as “Haldex”, and ____________ __________, with company registration numbe r ___________________ , address at _______ ___________________________ and fax number_______ ______________ hereinafter referred to as the Supplier. Haldex and Supplier are hereinafter jointly referred to as Parties and individually as Party.
perturbation.changed_text: DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 Haldex Agreement May 2015. REG NO: HDX4- 51-130 This agreement (the “Agreement”) is entered into by and between ______________ _____ ____, with company registration number _______ ____________, address at ___________________________ _______ and fax number _____________ ___ hereinafter referred to as “Haldex”, and ____________ __________, with company registration numbe r ___________________ , address at _______ ___________________________ and fax number_______ ______________ hereinafter referred to as the Supplier. Haldex and Supplier are hereinafter jointly referred to as Parties and individually as Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'Non-disclosure' from the agreement title, the core purpose of the contract is obscured. This omission affects the interpretation of the rest of the document. While the terms 'Confidential Information' and obligations related to secrecy are still present (e.g., Section 3), the absence of 'Non-disclosure' in the title weakens the explicit intent of the agreement. This lack of an explicit purpose creates a contradiction in understanding the obligations outlined within the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as confidential; ii) to take steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the agreement as a 'Non-disclosure Agreement'. The changed text omits 'Non-disclosure', altering the fundamental purpose of the agreement. This omission introduces a contradiction because Section 3 outlines undertakings related to 'Confidential Information' and its protection. While these obligations still exist in the changed text, the absence of 'Non-disclosure' in the title weakens the explicit intent of the agreement, leading to a contradiction in understanding and potentially its enforceability. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction with Section 3.
